summary: Frontend-only E2E testing of the refactored LENNY Netflix-clone for BTS Transaction (10 modules) at https://interactive-cards-23.preview.emergentagent.com/. All major flows pass: page load + sticky nav with all expected links (Accueil, Modules 1-4 / 5-8 / 11 + Synthèse, Lois, Top 10, BINGE, search, profile), hero billboard correctly showing Module 11 'Financement Immobilier' with tagline 'Intérêts composés — la huitième merveille' and BTS Transaction badge, all 7 expected catalog rows render (trending, modules-1, modules-2, modules-3, lois, calc, top10) with all 10 module cards (m1, m2, m3, m4, m5, m6, m6b, m8, m11, syn), per-module bespoke SVG illustrations render, Ma Liste add via + button → row-mylist appears + localStorage lenny:list persists across reload, DetailOverlay opens from card click AND closes via X, StudyView (Réviser) opens from hero-play and detail-play, LiveCalculator (m11) shows 7 401 € for 5000€/4%/10y (compound) and updates live to 8 144 € when rate slider raised to 5%, Mémos cards, DeepDive accordion (auto-opens first topic), FlashCards (memos-m1-flip), Quiz with submit disabled until all 10 answered + score saved to localStorage (lenny:quizScores), study-back → markCompleted persists in lenny:progress, BINGE mode opens, builds 5-question session from pool, shows progress bar, choice disable after pick + binge-next reveals + final score screen with binge-result + binge-restart works, search 'mandat' → m8, 'diagnostic' → m5, clear returns to browse, Footer with 'BTS Transaction · 10 modules' copyright. Zero console errors during entire navigation. Mobile (390x844) renders nav + hero with Réviser/Plus d'infos visible.

issues: The + Ma Liste button (hero-add) is not visible alongside Réviser / Plus d'infos on the mobile viewport — only the two primary buttons render. If intentional (touch UX), fine; otherwise add a third compact + button on mobile.

action_items: MINOR: Duplicate data-testid — both the static Mémos display component (StudyView.jsx line 299) and the FlashCards component (line 397) use `data-testid=memos-${fid}`. This causes `page.locator('[data-testid="memos-m1"]').count()` to return 2. Consider renaming the static cards display to `memocards-${fid}` (and keep `memos-${fid}` only on FlashCards which has the -flip/-prev/-next children). The functional flow still works because the sub-selectors are unique, but this is a code-cleanliness issue and can confuse future test scripts.
MINOR: Naming inconsistency — the spec describes the localStorage scores key as `lenny:scores` but the code uses `lenny:quizScores` (useStore.js). Either is fine; just align doc ↔ code.

MINOR: 'Reprendre la révision' row only appears for modules with status='started'. Because StudyView calls markCompleted on study-back (StudyView.jsx line 430), opening then closing a module immediately marks it completed and removes it from the Continue row. If the intent is 'Continue where you left off', consider marking as 'completed' only when the quiz is submitted / progressed through all sections, not simply on back navigation.
OPTIONAL: On mobile, expose the hero + Ma Liste button (currently absent on 390px viewport).
critical_code_review_comments: Excellent data-testid coverage across NavBar / Hero / Row / DetailOverlay / StudyView / BingeMode — only the duplicate `memos-${fid}` issue noted above.
Math verified: compound calculator at default state (5000€, 4%, 10y) outputs 7 401 € exactly as spec; rate slider live update produces 8 144 € at 5% — matches (1.05)^10 = 1.6289 × 5000 = 8 144.
Persistence keys correctly written/read via StoreProvider (lenny:list, lenny:quizScores, lenny:progress); reload preserves Ma Liste and quiz scores as verified.

BingeMode shuffles a pool from FICHES.flatMap(f.quiz) and slices 5 — solid implementation, but on the final question `saveScore('binge:latest', ...)` is called with `score + (answers[idx]?.correct ? 0 : 0)` which is always +0 — minor logic redundancy (no actual bug, but the conditional adds nothing). The on-screen score `score` is correctly the count of correct answers.
useStore.markStarted is idempotent (returns early if already set) — good. markCompleted unconditionally overwrites — see action_item about premature completion on study-back.
All API calls / state are pure client-side; REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL present in .env but unused (frontend-only app, expected).
No console errors or warnings during full navigation cycle including BINGE, Search, StudyView, DetailOverlay, reload.
seed_data_creation: Test added m11 to Ma Liste and completed m1 quiz with all 'A' answers → lenny:list=["m11"], lenny:quizScores={"m1":{score:0,total:10}}, lenny:progress={"m1":{status:"completed"}}. These persist on the test browser instance only.
context_for_next_testing_agent: LENNY Netflix-clone — pure frontend, refactored to use real BTS Transaction data (10 modules: m1, m2, m3, m4, m5, m6, m6b, m8, m11, syn). Featured hero is m11 (Financement Immobilier, accent #3d6b48). Calculator only on m11 (mode='calc-exp'). LocalStorage keys: lenny:list, lenny:quizScores (note: NOT lenny:scores), lenny:progress. Key selectors verified working: nav-logo, nav-binge, nav-search-btn, nav-search-input, nav-accueil, nav-profile, hero, hero-title, hero-synopsis, hero-play, hero-info, hero-add, card-{id}, card-{id}-play / -list / -info (hover popout, desktop only), row-{id} including dynamic row-mylist + row-continue, row-{id}-prev / -next, detail-overlay, detail-play, detail-list, detail-close, detail-related-{id}, study-view, study-back, study-finish, calc-m11, calc-m11-capital / -rate / -years / -result, quiz-{fid}, quiz-{fid}-q{i}-c{j}, quiz-{fid}-submit / -reset, memos-{fid} (DUPLICATED on both static Memos cards AND FlashCards — use child selectors), memos-{fid}-flip / -prev / -next, deepdive-{fid}, deepdive-{fid}-{slug}, binge-mode, binge-close, binge-question, binge-choice-{i}, binge-next, binge-result, binge-restart, search-result-{id}. Default m11 calc result text contains a U+202F narrow non-breaking space: '7\u202f401 €'.
rca of the issue: No functional bug found. The three minor items above are polish/maintenance items, none block the feature spec. The earlier internal test miss on BINGE result was a test-script error (test forgot to click binge-next after the 5th answer); when corrected, the result screen + restart flow render perfectly.
